The Farmer
  • API Reference
  • farmer.brick — Brick
  • View page source

farmer.brick — Brick

A Brick is a rectangular cutout of the survey mosaic at a fixed sky position. It aggregates data from multiple bands, runs source detection, manages source groups, and absorbs fitting results. Each brick is persisted to an HDF5 file under PATH_BRICKS.

class farmer.brick.Brick(brick_id=None, position=None, size=None, load=True, silent=False, tag=None)[source]

Bases: BaseImage

A spatial tile of the survey mosaic used for parallel source detection and photometry.

__init__(brick_id=None, position=None, size=None, load=True, silent=False, tag=None) → None[source]

Initialise a Brick, either by loading from disk or creating fresh.

A brick is a spatial sub-region of the full mosaic used for parallel processing. When load is True the object is populated from an existing HDF5 file named B{brick_id}[_{tag}].h5 in conf.PATH_BRICKS; otherwise a blank brick is built from the given position/size or looked up from the detection WCS via brick_id.

Parameters:

  • brick_id – Integer brick identifier (1-indexed on the conf.N_BRICKS grid). Mutually exclusive with providing both position and size.

  • position – astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord of the brick centre. Used only when brick_id is None.

  • size – (dec_height, ra_width) tuple of astropy.units.Quantity angles giving the brick size. Used only when brick_id is None.

  • load – If True, load an existing HDF5 brick file from disk. Defaults to True.

  • silent – If True, suppress informational log messages. Defaults to False.

  • tag – Optional string appended to the filename as _{tag}. Defaults to None.

Raises:

RuntimeError – If both brick_id and position/size are supplied simultaneously.

_condition_band_data(band)[source]

Sanitise a single band’s image arrays at ingest or load time.

Enforces three invariants required by the rest of the pipeline:

  1. The science array contains no NaN or Inf values (replaced with 0).

  2. The mask array is strictly boolean (non-zero → True).

  3. Weight values are non-negative and finite; pixels that are bad in science or masked are zeroed in the weight array.

Parameters:

band – Band name (key in self.data) to condition in place. Returns immediately if the band or its science array is absent.

_condition_all_bands()[source]

Sanitise every band currently registered on this brick.

Calls _condition_band_data() for each entry in self.bands. Used after loading a brick from HDF5 to ensure data integrity.

get_figprefix(imgtype, band)[source]

Generate filename prefix for output figures.

Parameters:

  • imgtype – Type of image (e.g., ‘science’, ‘model’, ‘residual’)

  • band – Band name

Returns:

Filename prefix in format ‘B{brick_id}_{band}_{imgtype}’

Return type:

str

get_bands()[source]

Get list of bands available in this brick.

Returns:

numpy array of band names

summary()[source]

Print a human-readable summary of this brick to stdout.

Reports the brick’s sky position, angular size (with and without buffer), the list of loaded bands, and per-band image statistics (sum, mean, median, std) and properties.

add_band(mosaic, overwrite=False)[source]

Cut out and attach a band from a full-field mosaic to this brick.

Takes a Mosaic object, extracts a Cutout2D for each image type (science, weight, mask, background, rms) centred on this brick including its buffer, and registers the PSF model list. Dummy zero-valued arrays are created for any image type not present in the mosaic. After ingestion, _condition_band_data() is called to sanitise the arrays.

Parameters:

  • mosaic – Mosaic object with pre-loaded image data for a single band.

  • overwrite – If True, re-add the band even if it already exists on the brick. Defaults to False.

Returns:

None. Returns early with a warning if the mosaic has no overlap with the brick footprint or if the band already exists and overwrite is False.

extract(band='detection', imgtype='science', background=None)[source]

Run source extraction on a band and store the resulting catalog.

Calls the base-class _extract method to detect sources, then masks sources that fall within the brick buffer zone (those detections that would be double-counted by adjacent bricks), adds id, brick_id, ra, and dec columns to the catalog, saves the segmentation map, and writes a DS9 regions file.

Parameters:

  • band – Band name to detect sources in. Defaults to 'detection'.

  • imgtype – Image type to run extraction on. Defaults to 'science'.

  • background – Pre-computed background array to subtract before detection. When None and the band’s subtract_background property is True, the background is fetched automatically. Defaults to None.

Returns:

None. Populates self.catalogs[band][imgtype], self.data[band]['segmap'], and self.n_sources[band][imgtype].

identify_groups(band='detection', imgtype='science', radius=conf.DILATION_RADIUS, overwrite=False)[source]

Group nearby sources by morphologically dilating the segmentation map.

Converts radius from arcsec to pixels using the detection WCS, calls dilate_and_group(), and stores the resulting group_id and group_pop columns in the catalog as well as the 2-D group map as a Cutout2D.

Parameters:

  • band – Band whose catalog and segmap to process. Defaults to 'detection'.

  • imgtype – Image type key in the catalog dictionary. Defaults to 'science'.

  • radius – Dilation radius as an astropy.units.Quantity angle. Defaults to conf.DILATION_RADIUS.

  • overwrite – If True, replace existing group_id/group_pop columns; if False, add them as new columns. Defaults to False.

spawn_group(group_id=None, imgtype='science', bands=None, silent=False)[source]

Instantiate a Group from this brick for a single source group.

Creates a Group object centred on the group’s bounding box, cuts out all requested bands, validates that segment pixel coordinates are in bounds, enforces the conf.GROUP_SIZE_LIMIT, transfers model catalog entries and tracking history from the brick, and returns the ready-to-process group.

Parameters:

  • group_id – Integer identifier of the group to spawn.

  • imgtype – Image type used to look up catalog group membership. Defaults to 'science'.

  • bands – List of band names to include in the group. None includes all bands available on the brick. Defaults to None.

  • silent – If True, suppress informational log messages. Defaults to False.

Returns:

Populated group object. group.rejected is True if

the group has no pixels, exceeds the size limit, or has out-of-bounds segment pixels.

Return type:

Group

detect_sources(band='detection', imgtype='science')[source]

Run the full source detection pipeline on this brick.

Calls extract() to detect sources and build the segmentation map, identify_groups() to assign group memberships, and transfer_maps() to propagate the maps to photometric bands. Optionally plots the science image when conf.PLOT > 1.

Parameters:

  • band – Band to run detection on. Defaults to 'detection'.

  • imgtype – Image type to process. Defaults to 'science'.

Returns:

The detection catalog for this brick.

Return type:

astropy.table.Table

process_groups(group_ids=None, imgtype='science', bands=None, mode='all')[source]

Model and/or measure photometry for all (or specified) groups.

Spawns each group in turn and passes it to run_group(). When conf.NCPUS > 1 and more than one group is requested, groups are processed in parallel via pathos.pools.ProcessPool with a lazy generator to keep peak memory usage low.

Parameters:

  • group_ids – Integer group ID(s) to process. None processes all groups in the detection catalog. Defaults to None.

  • imgtype – Image type used to look up catalog group membership. Defaults to 'science'.

  • bands – Band names to include in each group. None uses all bands on the brick. Overridden to ['detection'] when mode='pass'. Defaults to None.

  • mode – Processing mode forwarded to run_group() — one of 'all', 'model', 'photometry', or 'pass'. Defaults to 'all'.

absorb(group)[source]

Merge a processed group’s results back into this brick.

Accepts the (group_id, model_catalog, model_tracker) tuple returned by run_group() and copies each source’s model and tracking entry into the brick-level dictionaries. Group-level tracker entries (keyed 'group') are stored separately in self.model_tracker_groups.

Parameters:

group – Tuple (group_id, model_catalog, model_tracker) as returned by run_group().
